DEFENDANT FERNANDO BORJA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT IS GRANTED UNDER CCP 473.5(A) PROVIDED DEFENDANT PAYS PLAINTIFF'S REASONABLE COSTS AND ATTORNEYS' FEES IN OBTAINING THE DEFAULT. IF THE PARTIES ARE UNABLE TO AGREE ON THE AMOUNT, PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A MOTION AND THE COURT WILL DETERMINE THE AMOUNT. THE COURT'S RULING IS BASED ON THE LIBERAL POLICY FAVORING A DECISION ON THE MERITS. THE MOTION IS DENIED UNDER CCP 473(B) AS UNTIMELY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Appearances:
Michael Heath (931.4207) for defendants and moving parties
Andrew Dimitriou of Dimitriou & Associates (434.1144) for plaintiff and responding party

Argued and the Court modified its ruling as follows: sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to causes of action for fraud and alter ego. There is not sufficient clarity to state fraud or alter ego allegations against a significant number of these LLCs. Greater specificity is required as to the fraud and alter ego allegations. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600

MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
Off Calendar Jul-09-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FERNANDO BORJA'S MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT

Appearances:
Andrew Dimitriou of Dimitriou & Associates (434-1144) for plaintiff and responding party
Howard Olsen of the Law Offices of Michael Heath (931-4207) for defendant and moving party

This matter was not argued. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: the motion is granted under CCP 473.5(a) provided defendant pays plaintiff's reasonable costs and attorneys' fees in obtaining the default. If the parties are unable to agree on the amount, plaintiff may file a motion and the Court will determine the amount. The Court's ruling is based on the liberal policy favoring a decision on the merits. The motion is denied under CCP 473(b) as untimely. Order signed in open Court. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Reporter: Kimberly Jennings, CSR 11452
